name: cache

members.name: Cache

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update(key_states: Tensor, value_states: Tensor, layer_idx: int, cache_kwargs: dict[str, object] | None = None)
methods.summary:
Append `key_states` / `value_states` for `layer_idx` and return
the full cached key/value tensors.

parameters.name: key_states
parameters.description: New key projections of shape `(B, num_heads, T_new, head_dim)`.

parameters.name: value_states
parameters.description: New value projections of the same shape.

parameters.name: layer_idx
parameters.description: Index of the transformer layer this update belongs to.

parameters.name: cache_kwargs
parameters.description:
Extra per-cache arguments (e.g. rotary `cache_position`).
Ignored by the dynamic cache; reserved for parity with other
cache implementations.

returns.description:
The accumulated `(keys, values)` for `layer_idx`, each of
shape `(B, num_heads, T_total, head_dim)`.

methods.name: get_seq_length
methods.summary: Return the number of cached tokens for `layer_idx` (0 if empty).

methods.name: get_max_cache_shape
methods.summary: Return the maximum sequence length the cache can hold.

methods.name: reorder_cache
methods.summary: Reindex the cache along the batch dimension with `beam_idx`.
methods.extended:
Used by beam search to keep each beam's cache aligned after the
per-step beam permutation.  Mutates the cache in place.

methods.name: get_usable_length
methods.summary: Return how many cached tokens can be used given `new_seq_length`.
methods.extended:
For a bounded cache this clamps so that `previous + new` never
exceeds `get_max_cache_shape`; for an unbounded cache it is
simply the current sequence length.

parameters.name: new_seq_length
parameters.description: Number of new tokens about to be appended this step.

parameters.name: layer_idx
parameters.description: Layer whose current length is queried.

returns.description:
The count of already-cached tokens that remain usable once the
`new_seq_length` tokens are accounted for against the cap.

members.summary: Abstract base class for all key/value caches used in attention.
members.extended:
A cache stores, per transformer layer, the key and value projections of
every token seen so far so that an incremental (one-token-at-a-time)
forward pass only has to project the *new* token's key/value and attend
against the accumulated history — turning an $O(T^2)$ re-encode of
the whole prefix at every step into an $O(T)$ update.

Subclasses implement `update` (the per-layer write/read), plus the
length-introspection and beam-reordering hooks.  Concrete subclasses are
`lucid.utils.cache.DynamicCache` (the default growing cache),
`lucid.utils.cache.StaticCache` (a fixed pre-allocated buffer for
compiled decoding), and `lucid.utils.cache.EncoderDecoderCache`
(paired self- + cross-attention).

members.name: DynamicCache

methods.name: __init__
methods.summary: Create an empty cache with no layers populated.

methods.name: seen_tokens
methods.summary: Total number of tokens processed across all `update` calls.

methods.name: __len__
methods.summary: Number of layers currently held in the cache.

methods.name: __getitem__
methods.summary: Return the `(keys, values)` pair cached for `layer_idx`.

methods.name: __iter__
methods.summary: Iterate `(keys, values)` pairs in layer order.

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update(key_states: Tensor, value_states: Tensor, layer_idx: int, cache_kwargs: dict[str, object] | None = None)
methods.summary:
Concatenate `key_states` / `value_states` onto `layer_idx` and
return the grown key/value tensors.
methods.extended:
The first call for a layer seeds the entry; later calls
`lucid.cat` the new token(s) onto the sequence dimension.

parameters.name: key_states
parameters.description: New key projections of shape `(B, num_heads, T_new, head_dim)`.

parameters.name: value_states
parameters.description: New value projections of the same shape.

parameters.name: layer_idx
parameters.description: Index of the transformer layer this update belongs to.

parameters.name: cache_kwargs
parameters.description: Accepted for interface parity; unused by the dynamic cache.

returns.description:
The accumulated `(keys, values)` for `layer_idx`, each of shape
`(B, num_heads, T_total, head_dim)`.

methods.name: get_seq_length
methods.summary:
Number of cached tokens for `layer_idx` (0 if not yet seeded),
read directly from the accumulated tensor's sequence dimension.

methods.name: get_max_cache_shape
methods.summary: Always `None` — the dynamic cache is unbounded and grows on demand.

methods.name: reorder_cache
methods.summary:
Reindex every layer's keys/values along the batch dimension with
`beam_idx` (in place) to keep beams aligned after a beam permutation.

methods.name: reset
methods.summary:
Empty the cache in place so the same object can be reused for a
fresh sequence (clears every layer and the token counter).

methods.name: crop
methods.summary:
Truncate the cache to at most `max_length` tokens along the
sequence dimension (in place).
methods.extended:
A negative `max_length` drops that many tokens off the end.  Useful
for rolling the cache back (e.g. rejecting speculative tokens).  A
`max_length` at or above the current length is a no-op.

methods.name: batch_repeat_interleave
methods.summary:
Repeat every batch row `repeats` times along the batch dimension
(in place) — e.g. to fan one prompt out into `repeats` samples.

methods.name: batch_select_indices
methods.summary:
Keep only the batch rows named by `indices` along the batch
dimension (in place).

methods.name: to_legacy_cache
methods.summary: Convert to the legacy `tuple(tuple(key, value), ...)` format.
methods.extended:
Returns one `(key, value)` pair per layer, in layer order — the
representation older decoder `forward` signatures consumed before
`Cache` objects existed.

methods.name: from_legacy_cache
methods.summary: Build a `DynamicCache` from the legacy tuple format.
methods.extended:
`past_key_values` is a per-layer tuple of `(key, value)` tensors;
`None` yields an empty cache.

members.summary: A cache that grows its key/value tensors by concatenation each step.
members.extended:
Holds one key tensor and one value tensor per layer in `key_cache` and
`value_cache` (see the Attributes section).  The
first `update` for a layer seeds the
entry; subsequent updates concatenate the new token(s) along the sequence
dimension.  This is the eager default for autoregressive decoding.
examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.utils.cache import DynamicCache
>>> cache = DynamicCache()
>>> k = lucid.zeros(1, 2, 3, 4)   # (B, H, T, D)
>>> v = lucid.zeros(1, 2, 3, 4)
>>> ck, cv = cache.update(k, v, layer_idx=0)
>>> cache.get_seq_length()
3

attributes.name: key_cache
attributes.description: Per-layer accumulated keys, each `(B, num_heads, T, head_dim)`.

attributes.name: _seen_tokens
attributes.description:
Running count of tokens the cache has processed (tracked from
`layer_idx == 0`), exposed via `seen_tokens`.

members.name: EncoderDecoderCache
members.signature: EncoderDecoderCache(self_attention_cache: _KVCache, cross_attention_cache: _KVCache, is_updated: dict[int, bool] | None = None)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(self_attention_cache: _KVCache, cross_attention_cache: _KVCache, is_updated: dict[int, bool] | None = None)
methods.summary: Pair an existing self-attention and cross-attention cache.

methods.name: __len__
methods.summary: Number of decoder layers (from the self-attention cache).

methods.name: __getitem__
methods.summary: Return `(self_k, self_v, cross_k, cross_v)` for `layer_idx`.

methods.name: __iter__
methods.summary: Iterate `(self_k, self_v, cross_k, cross_v)` tuples in layer order.

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update(key_states: Tensor, value_states: Tensor, layer_idx: int, cache_kwargs: dict[str, object] | None = None)
methods.summary: Update the decoder **self-attention** cache for `layer_idx`.
methods.extended:
Cross-attention key/value are written directly through the
`cross_attention_cache` by the attention module (guarded by
`is_updated`), not through this method — so this delegates only to the
self-attention sub-cache.

parameters.name: key_states
parameters.description: New decoder self-attention keys, `(B, num_heads, T_new, head_dim)`.

parameters.name: value_states
parameters.description: New decoder self-attention values, same shape.

parameters.name: layer_idx
parameters.description: Index of the decoder layer this update belongs to.

parameters.name: cache_kwargs
parameters.description:
Forwarded to the self-attention sub-cache (e.g. a `StaticCache`
reads `"cache_position"` from it).

methods.name: get_seq_length
methods.summary:
Decoder sequence length for `layer_idx` (from the self-attention
sub-cache; the cross-attention length is fixed and not counted).

methods.name: get_max_cache_shape
methods.summary:
Maximum decoder length, delegated to the self-attention sub-cache
(`None` if it is a growing `DynamicCache`).

methods.name: reorder_cache
methods.summary:
Reindex both sub-caches along the batch dimension with `beam_idx`
(in place) so self- and cross-attention stay beam-aligned.

methods.name: reset
methods.summary: Empty both sub-caches and clear the cross-attention 'updated' flags.

methods.name: crop
methods.summary: Truncate the decoder self-attention cache to `max_length` tokens.
methods.extended:
The cross-attention cache is the fixed-length encoder memory and is
left untouched.

methods.name: batch_repeat_interleave
methods.summary: Repeat every batch row `repeats` times in both sub-caches.

methods.name: batch_select_indices
methods.summary: Keep only the batch rows named by `indices` in both sub-caches.

methods.name: to_legacy_cache
methods.summary:
Convert to the legacy per-layer `(self_k, self_v, cross_k, cross_v)`
tuple format.

methods.name: from_legacy_cache
methods.signature: from_legacy_cache(past_key_values: tuple[tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor], ...] | None = None)
methods.summary: Build an `EncoderDecoderCache` from the legacy 4-tuple format.

members.summary: Wraps a decoder self-attention cache and a cross-attention cache.

parameters.name: self_attention_cache
parameters.description: Grows by one position per decode step (decoder self-attention).

parameters.name: cross_attention_cache
parameters.description: Filled once from the encoder `memory` then reused unchanged.

parameters.name: is_updated
parameters.annotation: (dict[int, bool] or None, optional, keyword - only)
parameters.description:
Per-layer cross-attention "already filled" flags.  When `None`
(default) they are inferred from `cross_attention_cache`'s sequence
length — correct for eager use.  The compiled decode path passes them
explicitly (all True) because a re-wrapped `StaticCache` resets its
length counter, so length can no longer signal filled-ness.

attributes.name: is_updated
attributes.description:
Per-layer flag marking whether the cross-attention key/value for that
layer has already been computed; lets cross-attention skip the
redundant `memory` projection on every step after the first.

members.name: StaticCache

methods.name: __init__
methods.summary: Create an empty static cache with capacity `max_cache_len`.

methods.name: seen_tokens
methods.summary: Total number of tokens written (tracked from `layer_idx == 0`).

methods.name: __len__
methods.summary: Number of layers currently allocated.

methods.name: __getitem__
methods.summary: Return the full `(keys, values)` buffers for `layer_idx`.

methods.name: __iter__
methods.summary: Iterate the per-layer `(keys, values)` buffers.

methods.name: update
methods.signature: update(key_states: Tensor, value_states: Tensor, layer_idx: int, cache_kwargs: dict[str, object] | None = None)
methods.summary:
Write `key_states` / `value_states` into the fixed buffer at
`cache_position` and return a **filled-prefix view** of it.
methods.extended:
The write goes through the traceable `lucid.index_copy` (not
in-place `__setitem__`), so the decode forward stays compile-visible
and the stored buffer keeps a constant `max_cache_len` shape every step.
The *returned* keys/values are narrowed (dim 2) to the filled-prefix
width (the `read_len` in `cache_kwargs`) so attention attends over the
real keys, not the zero-padded tail — recovering O(filled) attention cost.

parameters.name: key_states
parameters.description: New key projections of shape `(B, num_heads, T_new, head_dim)`.

parameters.name: value_states
parameters.description: New value projections of the same shape.

parameters.name: layer_idx
parameters.description:
Index of the transformer layer this update belongs to (buffers are
lazily allocated on the first call for a layer).

parameters.name: cache_kwargs
parameters.description:
May carry `"cache_position"` (a fixed-shape int64 `Tensor` of
absolute write indices; absent → positions from the running counter)
and `"read_len"` (an int — narrow the returned view to this width;
the decoder-only attention passes `past_len + T` here to attend over
only the filled prefix).  When no `read_len` is given the full buffer
is returned (back-compatible).

returns.description:
The `(keys, values)` for `layer_idx`, narrowed on dim 2 to the
opted-in `read_len` when provided, else the full
`(B, num_heads, max_cache_len, head_dim)` buffer.  A narrowed view is
equivalent to the full buffer with the unwritten tail masked.

methods.name: get_seq_length
methods.summary:
Number of tokens written so far for `layer_idx`, read from the
per-layer counter (the buffer shape is always `max_cache_len`, so the
count cannot be inferred from it).

methods.name: get_max_cache_shape
methods.summary: Return the fixed buffer capacity `max_cache_len` (always bounded).

methods.name: reorder_cache
methods.summary:
Reindex every layer's buffers along the batch dimension with
`beam_idx` (in place) to keep beams aligned after a beam permutation.

methods.name: reset
methods.summary: Zero every buffer and reset the length counters in place.

methods.name: crop
methods.summary: Logically truncate the cache to `max_length` tokens.
methods.extended:
The buffer is fixed-size, so cropping only rewinds the length counters
(cropped positions become 'future' and are masked by the causal mask).
A negative `max_length` drops that many tokens off the end.

methods.name: batch_repeat_interleave
methods.summary: Repeat every batch row `repeats` times along the batch dimension.

methods.name: batch_select_indices
methods.summary: Keep only the batch rows named by `indices` along the batch dim.

members.summary: A bounded cache backed by fixed pre-allocated buffers written in place.

parameters.name: max_cache_len
parameters.description:
Maximum sequence length the buffers can hold.  Must not exceed the
model's positional range.

notes:
`get_seq_length` is tracked by a per-layer counter (not inferred from the
buffer shape, which is always `max_cache_len`).  `update` stores into the
full buffer; when the consumer opts in (passes a `read_len` in
`cache_kwargs`), it returns a **filled-prefix view** so attention attends
only over real keys — O(filled), not O(max_cache_len).  The stored buffer
stays full, so the fixed buffer shape is preserved.

attributes.name: max_cache_len
attributes.description: Fixed sequence capacity of every layer's buffer.

attributes.name: key_cache
attributes.description:
Per-layer key buffers, each `(B, num_heads, max_cache_len, head_dim)`
(lazily allocated on the first `update`).

summary: lucid.utils.cache: key/value caches for incremental attention (generation).
extended:
Class names, method names, and argument names mirror the reference
`cache_utils` module so that generation code ports across with only an
import change.  The eager default is `DynamicCache` (grows by
concatenation); `StaticCache` is a fixed pre-allocated buffer that keeps
shapes constant for compiled single-token decoding; and encoder-decoder models
use `EncoderDecoderCache` (paired self- + cross-attention).  Canonical
paths: `lucid.utils.cache.Cache` / `DynamicCache` / `StaticCache` /
`EncoderDecoderCache`.
